title: Prefer local-variable type inference (var) where appropriate
description: Encourages the use of local-variable type inference (the 'var' keyword) in Java where it improves code readability and maintainability. Using 'var' can make code more concise and reduce redundancy, but should be used judiciously to avoid loss of clarity.
recommendation: Use 'var' for local variable declarations when the type is obvious from the context, but avoid it when it would make the code less clear.
impact: Improves code readability and reduces verbosity, but overuse may reduce code clarity.

checks.message: Use 'var' for local variable declarations when the type is obvious from the context, but avoid it when it would make the code less clear.
